reasoning: The base rate for swimming world records remaining unbroken over short intervals is extremely high (~0.991). Lilly King has held the 100m breaststroke long course world record (1:04.13) since 2017, demonstrating extraordinary durability. As of the cutoff date (Oct 26, 2025), I confirmed she is still listed as the Wikipedia record holder. The next few months (October 2025 through early 2026) perfectly align with the typical short-course (25m pool) swimming season (including the heavily featured World Aquatics Swimming World Cup and Short Course World Championships), leaving very few long course (50m) opportunities for competitors to challenge the mark. Recent sub-1:04 efforts by rivals like Anna Elendt (1:03.83) or Ruta Meilutyte (1:02.77) have exclusively occurred in short course pools. Top rivals in long course (such as Qianting Tang and Meilutyte) are still approximately 0.2 to 0.5 seconds slower than King's mark. Barring a completely unexpected and staggering performance at a minor national meet, the record is exceptionally safe. I have set the probability at the maximum allowed safe boundary (0.95) for the nearest date and decayed it marginally for the later dates.
